mnemonics: S.P.L.I.T. Priority: Scheduling Policy, Load, Importance, Timeslice
keyPoints: Thread scheduling decides which ready thread runs next on a CPU core.
Schedulers use algorithms (like Round-Robin, Priority) to optimize for fairness, throughput, and response time.
A context switch occurs when the scheduler replaces the currently running thread with another.
commonMistakes: Assuming more threads always lead to better performance (can cause thrashing).
Confusing thread scheduling with process scheduling (threads share resources, processes do not).
